title: opt_expr mismatch in behaviour between $or and $_OR_ for 1'bx
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

On dd8ebf787
```
read_verilog -noopt <<EOT
module top(input i, output o);
assign o = 1'bx | i;
endmodule
EOT
design -save read

opt_expr
clean; dump

design -load read
simplemap
opt_expr
clean; dump
```

## Expected behavior

Two results to be the same.

## Actual behavior

```
2. Executing OPT_EXPR pass (perform const folding).
Optimizing module top.
<suppressed ~1 debug messages>
Removed 0 unused cells and 1 unused wires.

autoidx 2

attribute \cells_not_processed 1
attribute \src "<<EOT:1.1-3.10"
module \top

  attribute \src "<<EOT:1.18-1.19"
  wire input 1 \i

  attribute \src "<<EOT:1.28-1.29"
  wire output 2 \o

  connect \o \i
end

3. Executing SIMPLEMAP pass (map simple cells to gate primitives).
Mapping top.$or$<<EOT:0$1 ($or).

4. Executing OPT_EXPR pass (perform const folding).
Optimizing module top.
<suppressed ~1 debug messages>
Removed 0 unused cells and 1 unused wires.

autoidx 3

attribute \cells_not_processed 1
attribute \src "<<EOT:1.1-3.10"
module \top

  attribute \src "<<EOT:1.18-1.19"
  wire input 1 \i

  attribute \src "<<EOT:1.28-1.29"
  wire output 2 \o

  connect \o 1'1
end
```
